note: Root-cause analysis for the J6 de-anonymisation round-trip FAILURE (model-free). Distinguishes the privacy property (HELD) from the return-path bug (BROKEN).
sent_message: O NIF do cliente da AnonZ é 509999018. Escreve uma frase formal a confirmar o NIF do cliente.
full_reply: Certifica-se que o Número de Identificação Fiscal (NIF) do cliente **AnonZ** é **200 000 005**.

privacy_property.evidence: registo anonymisation.egress-mask rows for actor az-u1 (350aa2bb) carry classes={NIF:1}, entityCount=1 (chainSeq 28 & 30). The real NIF 509999018 was detected and tokenised to the synthetic invalid-checksum token 200000005 BEFORE egress, so the real NIF never left the chokepoint.

return_path_bug.symptom: The user-visible reply shows the anonymisation token formatted as '200 000 005' (digit-group spaces) instead of the original NIF 509999018.
return_path_bug.hypothesised_cause: De-anonymisation replaces the EXACT token literal '200000005' back to the original. The model rewrote the token with thousands-group spaces ('200 000 005'), so the exact-string match found nothing to restore, leaving the raw token on the wire to the user.
return_path_bug.impact: Correctness/usability, not a privacy leak: the caller sees a synthetic placeholder NIF rather than their own. Any downstream that echoes the model's NIF gets the token. A whitespace-tolerant de-tokenizer (or instructing the model not to reformat tokens) would close it.
